methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
HIMERA is a Seed-stage defense and security company founded by Misha Rudominsky and Oleksiy Oliynyk that has raised $2.5M+. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 10 private and 2 public companies in the Defense & Security sector, including Seed and Series A-stage peers.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $2M raised proxy as a baseline and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, implying a $6M valuation. Peer-median analysis examines five same-sector comparables—Tenna Systems ($60M at Seed), NODA AI ($125M), Ares Industries ($150M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Stark Defence ($500M)—yielding a $150M median valuation with a range of $60M to $500M.

Triangulating these methods produces a low estimate of $5M, a median of $78M, and a high of $172M. The wide range reflects uncertainty inherent in Seed-stage valuation and the substantial variance across peer series-stage positions. Stage-progression alone suggests HIMERA trades considerably below peer medians, while peer comparables suggest meaningful upside if the company executes through Series A. Key uncertainties center on technology differentiation, traction metrics, and whether peer valuations—particularly the $500M outlier—reflect market conditions or exceptional capability. Resolution of these factors will be critical to narrowing the range at the next funding event.
